{"text":"As someone who had an H1b Visa, it is actually stressful to get one. I know many friends who pursued their masters education and started working on their student visas but could not continue to do so since their student visas expired and they didn't get an H1b lottery pick. {"text":"No, it should be decentralised...so there is more accountibility and more reach to the actual residents of a certain area...if everything is under central government than it could lead to a dictatorship and even if the central leadership is good they won't be able to function properly since they would be disconnected on the ground level giving rise to anarchist mentality...this was the main issue with UPA govt... everything was being decided by 'Party High Command', even major decisions for states, UTs and major metropolitan Municipal corporations... Mumbai-pune-jaipur me metro banani hai, let Sonia Gandhi decide, not the chief minister of that state or the mayor of the city, but party president who would be sitting in Delhi...this is what happens with centralised power... central power in defence and security is fine, but for infra work or education, healthcare etc, local people and their govs should be able to decide"}
